name: Extracellular vesicle and secretome therapies surveillance pass for June 6, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Extracellular Vesicle and Secretome Therapies since the 2026-06-01 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a delta surveillance pass from the 2026-06-01T20:58:42Z public baseline publication through 2026-06-06. PubMed date-window searches found several close records, including a cardiac-derived EV brain-aging mouse paper, a PRP-centered facial rejuvenation split-face trial using an exosome-oriented photothermal protocol, a pediatric secretome wound case series, an APOSEC hydrogel formulation study, an MSC-exosome osteoarthritis review, and a retraction notice for an older diabetic-wound exosome mouse paper. None supplied a new product-defined EV or secretome human functional result, durable disease or mortality outcome, standardized product-characterization advance, registry-posted result, or correction to a baseline source that changes the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal outlook. ClinicalTrials.gov checks found the baseline Alzheimer's exosome registry still unknown status with no posted results, plus adjacent skin-aging, osteoarthritis, alopecia, autism, and neuroinflammation exosome or secretome registries without posted results or same-window aging-relevant evidence. Web and preprint checks did not identify a material public-source delta. No public outlook, source, study, finding, activity, bundle, or coverage assessment update is warranted.

materiality_decision.rationale: The public baseline already assigns low-confidence human_biomarker_signal because controlled human evidence is concentrated in local skin-aging biomarkers, while direct aging biology remains preclinical and disease-specific human functional evidence is small, uncontrolled, or null. Current PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, web, and preprint checks found no post-baseline result that changes stage, confidence, momentum, blockers, safety or durability interpretation, evidence-map evidence, or forecast criteria.

search_log.query: ((extracellular vesicle* OR exosome* OR secretome OR conditioned medium) AND (aging OR ageing OR rejuvenation OR senescence OR frailty OR healthspan OR longevity OR osteoarthritis OR Alzheimer* OR wound OR skin)), Entrez-date and publication-date window 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-06
search_log.summary: Returned 95 same-window indexed records. Close records included PMID 42243457 cardiac EV brain-aging mouse biology, PMID 42239964 pediatric secretome wound case series, PMID 42225690 PRP-centered facial rejuvenation trial, PMID 42157532 MSC/exosome OA review, PMID 42067172 APOSEC hydrogel formulation, PMID 42103525 diabetic-wound exosome retraction notice, and multiple preclinical wound, OA, skin, and neurodegeneration sources. None changed the public track interpretation.

search_log.query: ((mesenchymal stem cell* OR MSC OR adipose OR umbilical cord OR platelet) AND (extracellular vesicle* OR exosome* OR secretome) AND (clinical trial OR randomized OR phase OR patient* OR older OR aging OR osteoarthritis OR Alzheimer*)), Entrez-date and publication-date window 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-06
search_log.summary: Returned 33 same-window indexed records. The closest human or translational hits were the pediatric wound case series, the PRP skin rejuvenation trial, the OA MSC/exosome review, and preclinical or review-level disease studies. No new controlled EV or secretome aging-function result was found.

search_log.query: Supporting PMIDs 33728821, 25324650, 36396862, 37377400, 37859748, 37353884, 39465400, and 40500748 with retraction, erratum, expression-of-concern, or corrected-and-republished publication types
search_log.summary: Returned zero correction, retraction, erratum, or expression-of-concern records for the eight supporting baseline PMIDs.

Confirmed that the close hits are pathologic EV mechanism, uncontrolled or retrospective wound context, PRP-centered skin rejuvenation, review-level translation, formulation work, pre-baseline exploratory secretome or biomarker work, or non-baseline correction/retraction context rather than material new public support.

The baseline Alzheimer's exosome registry NCT04388982 remains unknown status with no posted results. Adjacent osteoarthritis, facial skin-aging, alopecia, autism, and secretome-combination registries are recruiting, completed without posted results, terminated without results, not-yet-recruiting, or wrong-scope. No registry-posted result or material same-window aging-relevant update was found.

search_log.query: (exosome OR extracellular vesicle OR secretome OR conditioned medium) AND (aging OR older OR osteoarthritis OR Alzheimer OR wound OR skin OR rejuvenation), plus MSC/exosome phase and clinical trial terms
search_log.summary: Returned exosome or secretome registries across stroke biomarkers, BPD, alopecia, osteoarthritis, neurodegeneration, skin/mucosal injury, autism, hearing loss, oral mucositis, and COVID contexts. No posted result changed the public evidence map.

search_log.query: Date window 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-06 with exosome, extracellular vesicle, secretome, and conditioned medium title or abstract terms
search_log.summary: Returned no matching bioRxiv or medRxiv preprints in the date window.

search_log.query: extracellular vesicle secretome aging clinical trial June 2026 exosome; mesenchymal stem cell exosome osteoarthritis trial June 2026 extracellular vesicle; secretome extracellular vesicle skin aging trial 2026 exosome rejuvenation
search_log.summary: Returned PubMed, ScienceDirect, ClinicalTrials.gov, review, and registry leads. Relevant leads were already checked directly or were pre-baseline context, review-level context, non-primary pages, or wrong-scope.

excluded_sources.title: Cardiac-derived extracellular vesicles carrying miR-4433b-3p accelerate cognitive decline and brain aging by suppressing TP53INP2-mediated neuronal autophagy in mice.
excluded_sources.reason: Material aging biology for EV-mediated intercellular signaling, but it identifies a pathologic aged cardiac EV cargo and mouse mechanism rather than an EV or secretome therapy result.

excluded_sources.title: Efficacy and safety of photothermal biomodulated platelet-rich plasma versus standard platelet-rich plasma for facial rejuvenation: a prospective split-face randomized study.
excluded_sources.reason: A same-window randomized split-face skin-rejuvenation study, but the tested intervention is PRP preconditioned with a photothermal protocol rather than a characterized EV or secretome product. It reinforces skin-aging biomarker activity without changing the public EV/secretome stage or evidence map.

excluded_sources.title: Adjunctive topical mesenchymal stem cell-derived secretome-enriched biological dressings in pediatric complex wounds: a retrospective case series.
excluded_sources.reason: Six-patient retrospective pediatric wound case series with standard-care co-treatment and no control group. It does not change the current controlled-human or disease-function evidence boundary.

excluded_sources.title: A comparative analysis of MSCs and their exosomes in osteoarthritis therapy: complementary roles and clinical translation.
excluded_sources.reason: Review article comparing MSCs and exosomes in OA. It reinforces manufacturing, regulatory, dosing, and long-term safety challenges but does not add primary efficacy evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Investigation of sterile hydrogels as topical vehicles for APOSEC, a stressed peripheral blood mononuclear cell secretome for the treatment of poorly healing wounds.
excluded_sources.reason: Formulation and delivery-system study for an APOSEC wound product; relevant to manufacturing and delivery but not a new human result or aging-function endpoint.

excluded_sources.title: Retraction notice to "Hypoxic ADSC-derived exosomes enhance wound healing in diabetic mice via delivery of circ-Snhg11 and induction of M2-like macrophage polarization" [Biomedicine & Pharmacotherapy 153 (2022) 113463].
excluded_sources.reason: Important literature-quality signal for diabetic-wound exosome work, but the retracted paper is not a supporting baseline source and does not directly alter the current public evidence map.

excluded_sources.title: Wharton's jelly mesenchymal stem cell-secretome enhances skin rejuvenation via ApoA4 and SERPINH1.
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-baseline exploratory secretome skin-rejuvenation source with in vitro work plus a one-week single-arm human pilot. It may be considered in a future coverage assessment, but ordinary surveillance should not retrofit historical source completeness when it does not change the current rating.

excluded_sources.title: MicroRNA profiles in plasma-derived extracellular vesicles across the human lifespan.
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-baseline human EV biomarker aging-cohort study, not a therapy. It may inform biology or biomarkers but does not support an intervention claim.

excluded_sources.title: the Safety and the Efficacy Evaluation of Allogenic Adipose MSC-Exos in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease
excluded_sources.reason: Baseline registry remains unknown status with no posted results and no current update.

excluded_sources.title: Mesenchymal Stem Cells Derived Exosomes in Osteoarthritis Patients
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting knee osteoarthritis exosome registry with no posted results and no same-window material change.

excluded_sources.title: Effects on Facial Skin Aging After Topical Application of Exosomes With a Microneedling Device
excluded_sources.reason: Completed facial skin-aging exosome registry with no posted results and no same-window material change.

excluded_sources.title: Exosomes for Hairloss Treatment
excluded_sources.reason: Terminated hair-loss exosome registry with no posted results. Hair growth and cosmetic dermatology context does not change the current track outlook.

excluded_sources.title: Investigating the Safety and Regenerative Potential of MSC-Derived Secretome Combined With PRGF in Knee Osteoarthritis
excluded_sources.reason: Not-yet-recruiting knee osteoarthritis secretome/PRGF registry with no results; trial-watch context only.

excluded_sources.title: GAIP ASD Research Study
excluded_sources.reason: New same-window registry involving umbilical cord blood-derived stem cells and exosomes, but the indication is autism spectrum disorder and it has no posted results.

notes: Triage selected this as the rank-1 next distinct surveillance target after the live-biotherapeutics publication.
The delta window was anchored to publication event publish-extracellular-vesicle-and-secretome-therapies-bootstrap-2026-06-01-2026-06-01t20-58-42-912z.

No coverage assessment exists yet for this track. This ordinary surveillance pass did not discover a material category-level change requiring immediate coverage repair, but the separate coverage-assessment backfill queue remains appropriate for historical source-completeness work.

The public boundary remains unchanged: human evidence is still strongest for local skin-aging biomarkers, direct aging biology is still mainly preclinical, and disease-specific human functional evidence remains exploratory, uncontrolled, null, or no-results.
next_actions: Continue ordinary surveillance for product-defined EV or secretome human functional results, especially controlled osteoarthritis, wound-healing, neurodegeneration, frailty, and skin-aging studies with standardized product characterization.
Watch ClinicalTrials.gov for posted results from NCT07372001, NCT06466850, NCT07157891, and related exosome or secretome registries.

Treat pre-baseline sources such as WJ-MSC secretome skin pilot and lifespan EV biomarker studies as coverage-assessment material rather than ordinary surveillance deltas unless a future public evidence-map refresh is requested.
Create a public bundle only if future evidence changes stage, confidence, momentum, blockers, safety, durability, taxonomy, or evidence-map wording.
